exemplar.title: âTo what extent did the fuel subsidy removal in Nigeria in 2023 negatively impact economic well-being in the nation?â

detailedExemplarData.title: âTo what extent did the fuel subsidy removal in Nigeria in 2023 negatively impact economic well-being in the nation?â

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The topic of fuelâsubsidy removal is communicated clearly and systematically around economic wellâbeing.
The research question is explicitly stated, wellâfocused on 2023 economic wellâbeing, and consistently referenced throughout.
A range of relevant sources (statistical agencies, peerâreviewed articles, media interviews) demonstrates informed selection.
weaknesses: The investigation occasionally drifts into broad social commentary, causing minor dilution of economic focus.
The methodology lacks primary data collection and specifics about sample sizes and interview protocols.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands lie in the top band (5â6) with only slight dilution of focus and methodological detail. The topic and research question are communicated effectively and consistently, and the source selection is appropriate. Although the methodology could be more specific, the overall performance aligns with the descriptor for a top mark of 6.

strengths: Graphs, tables, and quotations are tied directly to policy impacts, showing clear relevance.
Economic knowledge (costâpush inflation, Lorenzâcurve shifts) is explained clearly and coherently.
Terminology (e.g. âequilibrium quantityâ, âGini coefficientâ) is used accurately and consistently.
weaknesses: Some assertions (e.g. inequality shifts, subsidyâcost trends) lack empirical backing from primary data.
Reliance on older or singular sources limits breadth; more contemporary literature would strengthen coverage.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Application of source material and disciplinary knowledge sit at the upper end of the 3â4 band, while terminology usage clearly meets the 5â6 band. Averaging these performances yields a strong mark of 5, reflecting clear and coherent understanding with room for broader empirical support.

strengths: The majority of data and research activities are clearly relevant to the research question.
Analysis is mostly clear, linking rising fuel prices to economic wellâbeing with supported conclusions.
The argument is logically structured, moving from policy history through impact dimensions to evaluation.
weaknesses: Causality is asserted rather than empirically tested; no regression or counterfactual analysis is employed.
Evaluation of alternative explanations and data biases is present but brief, limiting critical depth.
A small portion of the discussion drifts into secondary commentary (e.g. protest hashtags) without economic linkage.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality and relevance align with the upper band of consistent linkage (6/12), analysis demonstrates clear but not fully sophisticated interpretation (7/12 band), and evaluation is reasoned but only partially critical (5/12 band). The combined performance justifies a holistic Critical Thinking score of 7.

strengths: The structure follows extendedâessay conventions (title page, abstract, TOC, numbered sections) clearly and effectively.
The table of contents and section headings facilitate rapid orientation.
Title and key elements (figures, tables) are consistently presented.
weaknesses: Page numbers are absent or inconsistently formatted.
Figure captions and citation styles are inconsistently applied.
Reference list mixes APA and adâhoc styles, and some images are low resolution.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the report structure fully meets the top band (3â4), layout and formatting show partial nonâcompliance (1â2). Overall, the balance of strengths and deficiencies supports a Presentation score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Strengths:** The research question is explicitly stated, concise, and anchored to a single time period (â2023â) and a single dependent construct (âeconomic well-beingâ), making it focused and easy to operationalise. It is repeatedly referenced in body sections and conclusion, giving a clear line of inquiry.  
**Weaknesses:** âEconomic well-beingâ remains broad and the essay treats many sub-indicators, so full precision is slightly weakened.  
Connection to the ensuing discussion is nevertheless strong; hence the work fulfils the top-band characteristics but not with complete precision, warranting a 5.

**Strengths:** The student clearly identifies a policy-focused topicâfuel-subsidy removal in Nigeriaâand consistently frames every section (history, impact analysis, evaluation) around economic well-being, demonstrating an appropriate and systematic focus for an Economics EE.  
**Weaknesses:** Although largely framed around âeconomic well-beingâ, the investigation occasionally drifts into broad social commentary without returning to economics explicitly, showing minor dilution of focus.  
Overall, communication is accurate and purposeful, with only slight over-breadth, fitting the 5â6 descriptor; a mid-band mark of 5 is most representative.

**Relevance:** Graphs, tables and quotations are almost always tied directly to the policy impact (inflation, fares, tuition), demonstrating appropriate application.  
**Gaps:** Some assertions (e.g., Lorenz-curve shift) are not backed by actual inequality statistics; a few references to protests are anecdotal. The essay occasionally cites secondary commentary where data would be preferable.  
Given mostly sound but not consistently rigorous application, the 3-4 band is met; a 4 best reflects the upper end of that performance.

**Structure:** The report follows EE conventionsâtitle page, abstract elements, table of contents, numbered sections, evaluation and conclusionâwhich are consistently applied.  
**Minor issues:** Occasional numbering inconsistencies and extra headings (e.g., repeated âimpactâ labels) slightly disrupt flow, yet overall readability remains high.  
Thus it fulfils the âclearly appropriateâ descriptor, meriting the top mark of the 3-4 band.

**Positive elements:** Word count, section headings, figures and tables are present, and citation style is attempted.  
**Deficiencies:** Page numbers are absent from the PDF captures, some images are low-resolution, and figure captions are inconsistently formatted; reference list mixing APA and ad-hoc styles shows partial non-compliance.  
These flaws mean only âsome layout considerationsâ are correctly applied, placing the work in the 1-2 band; a score of 2 acknowledges the reasonable but incomplete formatting effort.

**Argument quality:** The student builds a logical narrative (history â impact dimensions â evaluation) and provides some consideration of limitations and alternative explanations.  
**Evaluation weakness:** Critique of data gaps and policy alternatives is present but brief; there is limited weighing of opposing evidence, so evaluation is only partially critical.  
This matches the descriptor for 4-6; a mid-band score of 5 reflects clear reasoning with moderate critical depth.

**Depth of analysis:** The essay interprets graphs, connects rising fuel prices to cost-push inflation, and discusses labour-market impacts, leading to mostly supported conclusions.  
**Limitations:** Causality is asserted rather than tested; no regression or counterfactual analysis is employed, so analysis, while clear, is not âeffective and well-supportedâ in the highest-band sense.  
It therefore aligns better with the 7-9 bandâs lower threshold; a score of 7 reflects that the analysis is clear and largely supported but not sophisticated.

**Demonstrated knowledge:** The student explains supply-and-demand effects, cost-push inflation, and Lorenz-curve shifts accurately, showing clear disciplinary understanding.  
**Weaknesses:** Analysis sometimes becomes descriptive of Nigeriaâs context without integrating deeper micro-macro linkages, and no advanced econometric technique is used, so coherence is good but not exemplary.  
This places the work firmly in the 3-4 band; a score of 4 captures the generally clear but not fully sophisticated knowledge.

**Breadth and relevance:** The majority of the data (NBS, TradingEconomics, tuition statistics) directly addresses pre- and post-policy conditions, showing clear linkage to the RQ.  
**Shortcomings:** Heavy reliance on short-run data and secondary commentary means relevance is clear but not always âconsistentâ (e.g., protest hashtags cited without economic linkage).  
This matches the upper end of the 4-6 band; a full 6 is suitable because only a minority of material drifts off focus.

**Evidence of range:** Sources span statistical agencies (NBS, CBN), international data portals, peer-reviewed articles, and media interviews, indicating informed selection beyond textbook material.  
**Limitations:** The methodology section remains descriptive; primary data collection is absent and acknowledged as a limitation, and statistical tools are confined to simple trend comparisons, so selection is not consistently âeffectiveâ in an EE sense.  
Therefore the strand meets âgenerally relevant â¦ some evidence of informed selectionâ, aligning with the 3-4 band; the stronger side of that band (4) is appropriate.

**Terminology use:** Terms such as âcost-push inflationâ, âequilibrium quantityâ, âheadline vs core inflationâ, and âGini coefficientâ are used accurately and consistently throughout text, graphs and explanations.  
**Minor issues:** Occasional typographical slips do not impede accuracy.  
Overall terminology meets the top-band descriptor; a conservative 5 reflects slight lapses while retaining strong consistency.

annotations.comment: The explanation of transportation and manufacturing effects demonstrates solid economic knowledge.
The footnote clearly identifies TradingEconomics as the data source, supporting transparency.
The title clearly identifies the investigationâs focus on fuel subsidy removal and effectively sets the scope of the study.
A precise word count is provided, demonstrating compliance with the maximumâwordâlimit requirement.
Page numbering is missing from the footer, hindering navigation and detracting from formal presentation.
Fuelâprice graph uses USD/Litre but omits explanation of exchangeârate conversion; clarify currency basis and methodology.
Page numbers appear in the ToC but are absent on actual pages; ensure both are aligned for consistency.
The caption explains the shift clearly, but the graph itself lacks axis labels and units; add these for precision.
A stray overline (âÌ7â) appears in the footer instead of a page number; correct numbering conventions.
Listing both quantitative and qualitative data sources is appropriate, reflecting an informed mixed-methods design.
No rationale is provided for source selection or discussion of potential data biases; justify choices to strengthen validity.
The research question is prominent but fragmented over two lines; consider presenting it as a single block and linking it immediately to the essayâs thesis.
The table of contents is well structured with clear hierarchies, facilitating rapid orientation.
Indentation for subsections in the ToC varies; align nested entries consistently to improve visual coherence.
The background offers useful context but does not directly tie its details back to the research question; strengthen that connection.
The section relies heavily on a single source (Danladi Bashir, 2016), limiting breadth; integrate more contemporary or varied disciplinary literature.
The citation lacks a page number and consistent formatting; include full bibliographic details for clarity.
The supplyâdemand diagram is appropriately used to illustrate subsidy effects, enhancing conceptual clarity.
No data source is given for the figure; include a proper reference to TradingEconomics or NBS to verify data provenance.
The history table succinctly summarizes key policy moments, aiding reader comprehension.
The LaTeX equation appearing under the table caption is unrelated and should be removed; it distracts from data interpretation.
Claims about subsidy costs and debt trends lack specific citations; insert references to NBS or CBN reports for support.
Research question is explicit, but the thesis outlined thereafter is broad; propose a narrowly defined hypothesis to guide analysis.
The methodology summary lacks specificity regarding sample sizes, selection criteria, and interview protocols.
The literature review would benefit from an introductory roadmap outlining its thematic structure.
Citation of the â#EndBadGovernanceâ protest is interesting but lacks economic analysis; integrate it with monetary impacts.
Sectoral impacts are well described but lack critical comparison of source findings; synthesize differing viewpoints.
Reference to global market factors strengthens argument but lacks source citation; add data or literature for support.
Linking cost-push inflation back to broader economic well-being demonstrates insightful analysis.
The cost-push inflation discussion terminates mid-sentence; complete the analysis for clarity.
The Investopedia reference should include author, publication date, and URL to meet academic standards.
